agents.title: Agent Settings
agents.subtitle: Default parameters for new agents
agents.stylePreciseDesc: Stable and repeatable answers, almost always giving the same result.
agents.styleBalancedDesc: Accuracy-focused, natural expression, suitable for daily use.
agents.styleFlexibleDesc: Broad thinking, willing to provide answers from multiple perspectives.
agents.styleCreativeDesc: Bold and divergent, with new surprises every time.
agents.maxTokens: Max tokens per reply (0 = endpoint default)

xlab.description: Experimental field. Upcoming experimental features and early previews will appear here. Stay tuned.

skills.title: Skills

market.title: Skill Market
market.repoSaved: Repo saved, discovery result kept from cache
market.repoRemoved: Repo removed, discovery result kept from cache

skills.emptyDesc: Go to the Skill Market to pick the capabilities you need. They will appear here immediately after installation. Installing your first Skill will expand how HarnessClaw works.
skills.autoAppear: They will appear here after selection and installation
skills.noReposDesc: Add a GitHub repo first, then refresh discovery.
skills.checkRepoDesc: Check repo URL, branch, and scan path then refresh again.
skills.noSkillFoundDesc: No skills discovered yet. Check branch, scan path, or click 'Refresh Discovery' to pull latest content.

repo.title: Skill Repository
repo.desc: Enabled repos participate in discovery. Refreshing only updates the remote index; it won't touch your original repo. Fill in scan path to limit range. Proxy only affects pulling and refreshing.
repo.addDesc: Keep one main input to start adding. Branch, display name, scan path, and proxy are in advanced settings.
repo.branchDesc: Default is `main`, custom branches are supported.
repo.name: Display Name
repo.proxyDesc: When enabled, clone, fetch, and ls-remote will use this proxy address.
repo.browseByRepoDesc: Browse by repository, installed directly to current CLI by default

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.subtitle: View history or continue recent context.
sessions.noHistoryDesc: Start a new conversation and it will appear here.
sessions.noMatchDesc: Try different keywords to search again.

multiSelect.deleteConfirm: All messages and history for the {{n}} selected sessions will be permanently deleted and cannot be recovered.

delete.title: Confirm Delete Session?

deleteConfirm.batchDesc: All messages and history of the {{count}} selected sessions will be permanently deleted and cannot be recovered.

projects.title: Projects
projects.emptyDesc: Create a project to centralize sessions and deliverables into one workspace.

release009.name: v0.0.9 Release Wrap-up
release009.description: Bring release notes, regression verification, and bug fixes together so context and deliverables stay in one project.

sidebarRefine.name: Sidebar Interaction Refinement
sidebarRefine.description: Handle multi-select conversations, click targets, and overflow issues in one place to avoid bouncing between pages.

skillsOnboarding.name: Skills Onboarding Cleanup
skillsOnboarding.description: Refine first-run guidance, repository entry points, and the default flow so new users can understand the next step faster.

night.title: Gather your scattered thoughts
night.detail: Ask questions, attach files, or split complex tasks to HarnessClaw.

morning.title: Start your day with a clear question
morning.detail: New chats keep context, files, and approvals, ready for any task.

afternoon.title: Continue your work in this session
afternoon.detail: Add background, attach files, and let the Agent handle the rest.

evening.title: Wrap up your day with a quiet chat
evening.detail: Questions, attachments, and progress will stay here for later.

stages.engineSubtitle: Choose a primary LLM service as the core brain

engineOptions.openaiDetail: Supports GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, etc. Compatible with OpenAI protocol endpoints.
engineOptions.anthropicDetail: Supports Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Claude 3 Opus, etc. Native API.

profileOptions.devDetail: Focus on code, debugging and architecture. High reasoning effort by default.
profileOptions.researchDetail: Expert in data gathering, summarization and paper analysis. Balanced strategy.
profileOptions.opsDetail: Quick handling of daily tasks, syncs and data organization. Lightweight strategy.

items.dev1: Refactor this function to be more readable and add unit tests.
items.dev2: Troubleshoot this error log, locate the root cause and provide a fix.
items.dev3: Review this PR's design and boundaries, listing potential risks.
items.dev4: Add rate limiting and retries to this interface, with unit tests.
items.res1: Search for new RAG papers from the past week and summarize key points.
items.res2: Compare architecture and use cases of 3 leading vector databases.
items.res3: Review mainstream benchmarks in the Agent evaluation field.
items.res4: Track all papers published by this author in the last 6 months.
items.wri1: Write a 300-word teaser post for next week's product launch.
items.wri2: Rewrite this technical document for non-technical colleagues.
items.wri3: Write a concise and powerful README for this open-source project.
items.wri4: Organize this retrospective into a story-driven external post.
items.dat1: Analyze order data in this CSV for retention and outliers.
items.dat2: Parse this log into fields and create a trend chart of hourly calls.
items.dat3: Analyze these A/B test results and provide statistical significance.
items.dat4: Cluster these user feedbacks into 5 most noteworthy topics.
items.lif1: Plan a two-day family weekend trip around Hangzhou.
items.lif2: Give me adjustment suggestions for next week based on my activity and diet.
items.lif3: Compare the total cost-performance of these two flight+hotel packages.
items.lif4: List weekend evening activities suitable for spending alone quietly.
items.dai1: Organize today's meeting notes into To-dos and schedule for next week.
items.dai2: Summarize all @ mentions in Slack today, marking those needing reply.
items.dai3: Organize this week's work into a draft weekly report for external use.
items.dai4: Pick 3 things from my emails that truly need processing today.

summary.waiting: Plan approved, waiting to dispatch · {{total}} steps total

header.title: Quick Launcher
header.subtitle: Global hotkey to summon the input bar and start a new chat from your question

enabled.label: Enable
enabled.desc: When enabled, the global hotkey opens the Quick Launcher input box; type a question to jump straight into a new chat.

hotkey.label: Hotkey
hotkey.desc: Click the input on the right and press a key combination to update. Include at least one modifier (⌘/⌃/⌥/⇧).
hotkey.capturingAria: Recording hotkey, press a key combination

stages.emmaSubtitle: Start with an Agent console that can both chat and work.
stages.engineSubtitle: Determine the model API protocol for the first connection.

engineOptions.openaiDetail: Connect to OpenAI official API or accounts compatible with its model naming and gateway configuration.
engineOptions.anthropicDetail: Connect to Anthropic official API as the default entry point for Claude series models.
engineOptions.xunfeiDetail: Connect to iFlytek Spark, suited for Chinese-language scenarios and the domestic developer ecosystem.
engineOptions.googleDetail: Connect to Google Generative Language for Gemini-series models via the gemini protocol.
engineOptions.deepseekDetail: Connect to DeepSeek's OpenAI-compatible API — great for code and reasoning at low cost.
engineOptions.zhipuDetail: Connect to Zhipu GLM via the OpenAI-compatible gateway; supports GLM-4 series models.
engineOptions.moonshotDetail: Connect to Moonshot Kimi via the OpenAI-compatible API for long-context conversations.
engineOptions.minimaxDetail: Connect to MiniMax via the OpenAI-compatible API; supports the abab series.
engineOptions.customDetail: Bring your own gateway — set base URL, key and a protocol (OpenAI or Anthropic) yourself.

profileOptions.devDetail: Focused on long-chain execution, with higher tool iteration limits by default.
profileOptions.researchDetail: Focused on multi-round retrieval and organization, maintaining medium reasoning intensity.
profileOptions.opsDetail: Focused on stable collaboration and batch processing, maintaining concise default values.

welcome.modelIdHint: Use a model ID supported by your provider, e.g. gpt-4o-mini or claude-sonnet-4.

items.dev1: Review this code for performance bottlenecks
items.dev2: Port this Python script to TypeScript
items.dev3: Write unit tests for this API
items.res1: Summarize recent LLM benchmark papers from the past 30 days
items.res3: List key feature differences across competitors
items.res4: Extract the key points from this meeting transcript
items.wri1: Rewrite this technical post for a broader audience
items.wri2: Draft a product launch announcement email
items.wri3: Suggest three catchy titles for this blog post
items.wri4: Turn these notes into a 200-word talk
items.dat1: Find the outliers in this CSV
items.dat2: Write a SQL query for last month's active users
items.dat3: Aggregate these logs into a visualization
items.dat4: Clean this dataset and fill missing values
items.lif1: Plan a 3-day getaway near my city
items.lif2: Suggest healthy breakfast ideas for the week
items.lif4: Recommend a few highly rated non-fiction books
items.dai1: Organize today's tasks into time blocks
items.dai3: Remind me to archive project progress every Friday
items.dai4: Transcribe this voice note into a meeting summary
